zh-tw.title: 夏天電費語錄
zh-tw.description: 六月一號夏月電價開跑，冷氣是空氣，電費是現實——獻給每個拆帳單前要先深呼吸的人。

en.title: Summer Electric Bill Quotes
en.description: Summer rates just kicked in. AC is oxygen, the bill is reality — for everyone who needs a deep breath before opening the envelope.

items.sourceNote: 夏月電價 6/1 開跑 三級距 2.55 元；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
六月一號，夏月電價正式啟動。
台電：「祝您有個涼爽的夏天。」
我：「妳這句話聽起來不像祝福，比較像收據。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛收到 6 月份電費通知的同事，一起認命
variations: 台電的夏天，是從你點開 APP 的那一刻開始的。
夏月電價的「夏」字，唸起來特別像「下重手」的「下」。

en.content:
June 1st: summer electricity rates officially begin.
Power company: 'Wishing you a cool summer.'
Me: 'That doesn't sound like a wish, it sounds like a receipt.'
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who just got their first summer bill notification — pure solidarity
variations: Summer doesn't start with the solstice. It starts the moment you open the utility app.
They call it 'peak season'. I call it 'please season — please use less'.

items.sourceNote: 冷氣 26 度 vs 28 度 妥協 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
台電說：「冷氣設 26 度最舒適，調高一度省 6%。」
我：「那我設 28 度，多出來的兩度，我用流汗補。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 貼在家裡客廳冷氣旁邊，給每天都在跟自己討價還價的你
variations: 省電方法：把冷氣調到 28 度。省更多的方法：把冷氣關掉，搬去便利商店住。

en.content:
Energy tip: 'Set your AC to 78°F for the perfect balance.'
Me: 'I set it to 80°F. The missing 2 degrees, I make up with sweat.'
en.editorNote: For anyone negotiating with their own thermostat every single night
variations: How to save on cooling: raise the thermostat. How to save more: move into a convenience store.

items.sourceNote: 「冷氣開整天電費才一兩千」鄉民炸鍋；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
有人說：「冷氣開整天，一個月才一兩千啊，何必省。」
我：「對，問題是那兩千，是我這個月唯一不繳的東西，叫做存款。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 回敬給那個跟你說「電費沒多少」的有錢朋友
variations: 「電費才幾千塊」這句話，是衡量一個人經濟段位的精準指標。

en.content:
Someone said: 'AC running 24/7 only costs like sixty bucks, why bother saving?'
Me: 'Right. That sixty bucks is the only thing I wasn't paying this month. We call it savings.'
en.editorNote: A polite response to anyone who tells you 'electricity isn't that expensive'
variations: 'Electricity isn't that bad' is the most accurate net-worth indicator ever invented.

items.sourceNote: 兩個月一張帳單 心痛兩個月份 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
別的痛苦都是一次一次來，
只有電費，是兩個月份的傷害，濃縮成一個信封一次給你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合每隔兩個月拆帳單前的心理建設，傳給室友一起做心理準備
variations: 夏天的電費單，是一種「兩個月的因，一秒鐘的果」。

en.content:
Most pain comes in single doses.
The electric bill is the only pain that lets two months ferment, then hands it to you in one envelope.
en.editorNote: Pre-bill therapy for anyone splitting utilities with a roommate
variations: Two months of cause. One envelope of consequence.

items.sourceNote: 蓋棉被吹冷氣 台灣特有 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
蓋棉被吹冷氣，
是一個人類為了把錢花得最沒效率，所能做出的最頂級操作。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給昨晚冷氣 22 度蓋兩條被子的那個朋友，他懂
variations: 蓋棉被吹冷氣，是台灣人對「自由」的最終定義。

en.content:
Cranking the AC to 65 and burrowing under a duvet:
the most premium way a human being can waste money on purpose.
en.editorNote: For the friend who slept under three blankets last night with the AC blasting
variations: AC at 65, duvet to the chin. Freedom, but make it expensive.

items.sourceNote: AC vs salary comparison meme；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
GDP 創新高，台股創新高，電費也創新高，
只有我的薪水，年年保持初心，從不隨波逐流。
zh-tw.editorNote: 貼在公司茶水間冷氣旁，跟同事用眼神默默乾杯
variations: 我家什麼都漲，只有我的存款堅守原地，像個老派紳士。

en.content:
GDP hits new highs. The stock market hits new highs. The electric bill hits new highs.
Only my salary stays loyal to its origins, refusing to follow trends.
en.editorNote: Print this and tape it next to the office AC for the silent eye-contact toast
variations: Everything in my life is appreciating. Just not my paycheck.

items.sourceNote: 去便利商店逛超久 蹭冷氣 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我去便利商店繞了 40 分鐘只買一瓶水。
店員：「在找什麼？」
我：「找一個不用我付電費的夏天。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合下午四點熱到不想回家的時候，配一張便利商店冷藏櫃的照片
variations: 圖書館、便利商店、銀行大廳——夏天三大公益冷氣聖地。

en.content:
I spent 40 minutes in 7-Eleven and bought one bottle of water.
Clerk: 'Looking for something?'
Me: 'Yeah. A summer somebody else is paying the AC for.'
en.editorNote: For that 4PM moment when you can't bring yourself to go back home
variations: Libraries, convenience stores, bank lobbies — the holy trinity of free summer AC.

items.sourceNote: AC is the only one who understands me；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
成年後最穩定的關係，是我跟我家那台冷氣。
它從不問我今天過得怎樣，但只要我按下遙控器，它就會用涼風溫柔地接住我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個說「冷氣是我老公」的朋友，她會泛淚
variations: 我跟我的冷氣已經到了不用解釋的階段，按下開機，它就知道今天不行。

en.content:
The most stable relationship I've had as an adult is with my AC unit.
It never asks how my day was, but the moment I hit the remote, it catches me with a soft cold breeze.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who introduces her air conditioner as her husband
variations: My AC and I are past the words stage. I press the button, it just knows.

items.sourceNote: 電費跨級距 帳單超過 700 度 痛苦倍增；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
上個月用 329 度，平安無事。
這個月用 331 度，台電當場升我一個等級——
從「節能模範生」直接畢業成「環境破壞首要嫌疑人」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 解釋給那個第一次發現自己跨級距的新室友聽，他需要被安慰
variations: 電費級距，是一條看不見的線。踩到的那一秒，你的人生瞬間貴了 25%。

en.content:
Last month: 329 kWh. Smooth sailing.
This month: 331 kWh. The power company instantly upgrades me — 
straight from 'Energy Honor Roll' to 'Person of Interest in Climate Damage'.
en.editorNote: Explain this to the new roommate who just discovered tiered billing
variations: There's an invisible line in your bill. The second you cross it, your life is 25% more expensive.

items.sourceNote: AC remote control fight register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我家冷氣遙控器，是世界上權力最集中的物品。
誰拿到它，誰就決定今晚這個家是熱帶雨林還是北極。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合家族群組，配一張遙控器的照片，讓爸媽自動對號入座
variations: 媽媽：26 度。爸爸：23 度。我：偷偷把遙控器藏到自己房間。

en.content:
The AC remote is the most concentrated form of political power in our house.
Whoever holds it decides whether tonight's climate is rainforest or Arctic Circle.
en.editorNote: Drop this in the family chat with a photo of the remote — let everyone self-identify
variations: Mom: 78. Dad: 70. Me: secretly relocates the remote to my own room.

items.sourceNote: summer paycheck disappears to bills register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
夏天的薪水組成：
房租 40%，電費 25%，吃飯 20%，奶茶 15%，存款請另尋他人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 貼在記帳 App 上方截圖，傳給也在算這條算式的朋友
variations: 夏天的存款，跟我家樓下那棵樹一樣——存在，但很瘦。

en.content:
Summer paycheck breakdown:
Rent 40%, electric 25%, food 20%, iced drinks 15%, savings please contact someone else.
en.editorNote: Pair with a screenshot from your budgeting app — caption inevitable
variations: My summer savings, like the tree outside my window: exists, but skinny.

items.sourceNote: 變頻冷氣 開整天 比較省 鄉民迷思；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
鄉民：「變頻冷氣開整天比較省。」
台電：「不是。」
鄉民：「但我相信我自己。」
月底：帳單寄到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每天用「我聽說」開頭的家庭群組長輩
variations: 省電的方法，全網路有 100 種版本。會收帳單的，只有你。

en.content:
Internet: 'Leaving inverter AC on all day actually saves more.'
Power company: 'No, it doesn't.'
Internet: 'But I believe in myself.'
End of month: bill arrives.
en.editorNote: For the uncle in the family chat who starts every message with 'I heard…'
variations: There are 100 versions of the AC-saving trick online. Only one person gets the bill.

items.sourceNote: open window or AC debate；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
媽：「開窗就好，自然風比較健康。」
我：「外面 36 度。」
媽：「那把窗戶關起來，冷氣別開那麼低。」
結論：媽媽永遠是對的，跟物理無關。
zh-tw.editorNote: 貼在 LINE 媽媽群組會炸鍋，建議只傳給知道笑點的朋友
variations: 媽媽的省電哲學第一條：你穿少一點就不用冷氣了。第二條：但不能太少。

en.content:
Mom: 'Just open the window, fresh air is healthier.'
Me: 'It's 97°F outside.'
Mom: 'Then close the window, but don't run the AC so low.'
Conclusion: Mom is always right. Physics is irrelevant.
en.editorNote: Will absolutely detonate the family chat — share with select friends only
variations: Mom's energy-saving rule #1: wear less, you won't need AC. Rule #2: but not too little.

items.sourceNote: opening the bill envelope dread；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
拆夏天電費單的步驟：
1. 深呼吸。
2. 跟自己說我有努力省電。
3. 拆開。
4. 把它折回去當作沒看過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每兩個月要進行一次這個儀式的所有人
variations: 夏天的電費單，是一種需要心理諮商陪同拆開的文件。

en.content:
How to open your summer electric bill:
1. Deep breath.
2. Tell yourself you tried to save.
3. Open it.
4. Fold it back and pretend you didn't see.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who performs this ritual every two months
variations: The summer electric bill is the kind of document you should open with a therapist on standby.

items.sourceNote: office AC vs home AC 對照 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
公司冷氣：22 度，免費，我披外套。
家裡冷氣：28 度，自己付，我穿背心還是熱。
結論：上班其實是去蹭冷氣，順便把錢領回家繳電費。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合 5 點下班前傳給同事，互相安慰一下
variations: 辦公室的冷氣是別人家的冷氣，永遠最涼。
上班最大的福利，是公司願意把冷氣開到我家不敢開的溫度。

en.content:
Office AC: 72°F, free, I'm wearing a hoodie.
Home AC: 82°F, on my dime, I'm in a tank top and still sweating.
Conclusion: I'm not going to work. I'm going to free AC and bringing home a paycheck to pay my own bill.
en.editorNote: Send to coworkers right before 5PM clock-out — group therapy
variations: Office AC is always colder, because it's not your bill.
The real perk of having a job is they crank the AC to a temperature you'd never dare at home.

items.sourceNote: PTT 鄉民討論除濕機 vs 冷氣 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
鄉民推文：「先開除濕機，再開冷氣，會比較省。」
我照做。
結果：除濕機跑兩小時，房間從烤箱變成蒸籠。
冷氣補上去：帳單從崩潰升級成絕望。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每年都會在群組轉發「省電 10 招」的長輩
variations: 省電撇步的最終結局，是兩台機器一起運轉，一起讓你破產。
除濕機跟冷氣的關係，就像我跟咖啡——說好只開一個，最後兩個都上。

en.content:
Internet tip: 'Run the dehumidifier first, then the AC. You'll save money.'
I tried it.
Result: two hours later, the room went from oven to sauna.
AC kicks in: my bill upgraded from devastated to hopeless.
en.editorNote: Send to the relative who forwards '10 ways to save power' every June
variations: Every energy-saving hack ends the same way: two machines running, one wallet crying.
Me and my dehumidifier had a deal: just one of you tonight. We both know how it ended.

items.sourceNote: 冷氣搭電風扇省電 官方建議 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
官方建議：冷氣搭電風扇，可以省 10%。
我照做。
現在我家有三台電風扇、一台冷氣、一台循環扇，全部一起轉。
總電費：原本的 130%。
zh-tw.editorNote: 貼在家裡那個風扇陣列前，紀念你失敗的省電大計
variations: 省電的訣竅，是不要加裝任何「輔助省電」的家電。

en.content:
Official tip: pair the AC with a fan, save 10%.
I tried it.
Now my apartment has three pedestal fans, one AC, and one air circulator, all spinning at once.
Total bill: 130% of normal.
en.editorNote: Tape this above your fan-army for a memorial to your failed savings plan
variations: The real way to save on cooling is to not buy any additional 'energy-saving' appliances.

items.sourceNote: 家裡寵物吹冷氣 鄉民討論；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
出門上班前的對話：
我：「冷氣關掉太熱。」
我：「但開著我會心痛。」
貓（瞇眼）：「你說什麼？」
冷氣：26 度，整天開到我下班。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家有毛小孩、夏天電費比自己生活費還高的奴才們
variations: 夏天的冷氣不是給人吹的，是給貓吹的，我只是付錢的房客。
養貓之後我才知道，原來電費可以為了一隻不會說謝謝的生物自願多付。

en.content:
Pre-work conversation:
Me: 'Turning off the AC feels cruel.'
Me: 'Leaving it on feels expensive.'
Cat (squints): 'I'm sorry, what?'
AC: 78°F, on until I get home at 7.
en.editorNote: For every pet parent whose summer bill exceeds their grocery budget
variations: The summer AC isn't for me. It's for the cat. I'm just the tenant who pays rent.
Becoming a pet parent means voluntarily paying extra electric for something that won't even say thanks.

items.sourceNote: PTT WomenTalk 夏季電費加乘抱怨 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
同居後最大的成長，是學會在冷氣開到 24 度時，平靜地說：
「沒事，我們各自分攤一半。」
心裡 OS：「但我用 27 度也活得下去，你知道嗎。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給剛跟另一半同居、開始討論分攤電費的朋友
variations: 感情會不會變淡，看電費單就知道。

en.content:
The biggest growth from moving in together is learning to calmly say,
'No problem, we'll split it 50/50,'
while internally screaming: 'But I could survive at 80°F, you know.'
en.editorNote: For couples who just started splitting the utility bill
variations: Want to know if a relationship will last? Check the summer thermostat negotiation.

items.sourceNote: AC compressor sound envy meme register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
夏天的鄰居關係，是一種奇怪的競賽：
聽到對面那家冷氣外機 24 小時運轉，我心裡浮現的不是抱怨，
是：「他們竟然付得起。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合住公寓、能聽見隔壁冷氣外機嗡嗡聲的所有人
variations: 判斷鄰居家庭月收入的最準方法，就是聽他家冷氣開多久。

en.content:
The strangest summer competition is with your neighbors:
Hearing their AC compressor humming 24/7, the first thought isn't 'how annoying.'
It's: 'how can they afford that.'
en.editorNote: For everyone in an apartment listening to the next-door AC unit drone all night
variations: Want to estimate your neighbor's salary? Just time how long their AC runs.

items.sourceNote: 破紀錄高溫 熱到融化 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
氣象署：「今天破紀錄高溫，請民眾注意防曬。」
我：「我注意了，所以我整天躲在冷氣房。」
台電：「那我也注意了，你的帳單。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一看到高溫特報就先嘆氣的同事，他懂這條食物鏈
variations: 天氣愈破紀錄，我的帳單就愈想破我的紀錄。
今年夏天唯一跟著創新高的，除了氣溫，就是我的電費。

en.content:
Weather service: 'Record-breaking heat today, please stay protected from the sun.'
Me: 'I am. That's why I've been hiding in the AC all day.'
Power company: 'And I'm protecting something too — your bill.'
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who sighs the moment a heat advisory pops up — they get the food chain
variations: The more records the weather breaks, the harder my bill tries to break mine.
The only thing hitting new highs this summer besides the temperature is my electric bill.

items.sourceNote: 在自己家流汗 矛盾 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
為了省電費，我選擇在自己家裡流汗。
為了流汗，我每天多喝兩瓶水。
為了那兩瓶水，我又多花了錢。
資本主義的盡頭，是你怎麼省都會被扣到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個堅持不開冷氣、結果飲料錢比電費還高的朋友
variations: 省下的電費，剛好等於我為了不開冷氣而多買的飲料錢。
在自己家流汗，是現代人最划不來的免費活動。

en.content:
To save on electricity, I chose to sweat inside my own home.
To sweat, I drink two extra bottles of water a day.
To buy that water, I spend more money.
The end of capitalism is realizing every way you save still costs you.
en.editorNote: For the friend who refuses to run the AC and somehow spends more on cold drinks
variations: The money I saved on AC exactly equals what I spent on drinks to survive without it.
Sweating in your own home: the worst-value free activity known to modern man.

items.sourceNote: 冷氣偏偏夏天壞掉 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
冷氣很有職業道德：
它從不在冬天壞掉。
它總是挑全年最熱的那一天，在你最需要它的那一刻，安靜地放棄人生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個冷氣在連假第一天罷工、約不到師傅的苦主
variations: 冷氣壞掉的時間點，跟你薪水入帳的時間點，永遠完美錯開。
夏天冷氣壞掉，維修師傅的行事曆，比演唱會門票還難搶。

en.content:
My AC has incredible work ethic:
it never breaks in winter.
It always picks the hottest day of the year, the exact moment you need it most, and quietly gives up on life.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose AC died on day one of a long weekend with no technician available
variations: The day my AC breaks and the day my paycheck lands are forever perfectly out of sync.
Booking an AC repair guy in July is harder than scoring concert tickets.

items.sourceNote: 非夏月最後一天 把握吹冷氣 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
五月三十一號的晚上，我把冷氣開到最強。
不是因為熱，
是因為明天開始就是夏月電價，今晚的每一度電，都是我用便宜價格能買到的最後溫柔。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在 5/31 當晚發限動，配一張冷氣遙控器特寫，全台一起倒數
variations: 五月的最後一夜，是非夏月電價的畢業典禮，請好好珍惜。
六月一號之後，吹冷氣不再只是消暑，而是一種需要勇氣的消費。

en.content:
On the night of May 31st, I crank the AC to max.
Not because it's hot —
because tomorrow summer rates begin, and every unit tonight is the last bit of cheap tenderness I can still afford.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a May 31 story post with a close-up of the AC remote — the whole island counting down together
variations: The last night of May is graduation day for off-season rates. Cherish it.
After June 1st, running the AC stops being relief and becomes an act of courage.

items.sourceNote: 學生宿舍 投幣冷氣 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
宿舍投幣冷氣的規則很簡單：
投十塊，撐三小時，睡四小時。
所以每天凌晨三點，全宿舍會準時響起一片「啊好熱」的合唱，像鬧鐘，但更絕望。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個住投幣冷氣宿舍、半夜被熱醒還要爬起來投錢的同學
variations: 投幣冷氣教會我的事：金錢買得到睡眠，但只有三小時的份。
宿舍冷氣斷電的瞬間，是當代年輕人最真實的午夜驚魂。

en.content:
The rule for coin-operated dorm AC is simple:
drop a coin, get three hours, sleep four.
So every night at 3 AM the whole floor breaks into a synchronized chorus of 'ugh, so hot' — like an alarm clock, but more hopeless.
en.editorNote: Send to the dormmate who gets jolted awake at 3 AM to feed coins into the AC
variations: Coin-operated AC taught me money can buy sleep — but only three hours of it.
The moment the dorm AC clicks off is the most authentic midnight horror of being young and broke.

items.sourceNote: 2026 節電獎勵金 每省一度 0.6 元 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
台電：「跟去年同期比，每省一度電，回饋你 0.6 元。」
我：「所以我要先記得去年的我用了多少度，再努力贏過他。」
結論：今年夏天我最大的對手，是去年那個不懂事亂吹冷氣的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個認真研究節電獎勵、把帳單當成電玩排行榜在打的朋友
variations: 省電獎勵金的精髓：跟過去的自己比賽，輸了還要付錢。
0.6 元一度，聽起來很少，但對一個月底只剩零頭的人來說，那是尊嚴。

en.content:
Power company: 'Beat last year's usage and we'll pay you back 60 cents per unit saved.'
Me: 'So first I have to remember how much past-me used, then defeat him.'
Conclusion: my biggest rival this summer is last year's me, who cranked the AC like a fool.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who treats the energy-saving rebate like a video-game leaderboard
variations: The rebate program in a nutshell: race your past self, and if you lose, you pay.
60 cents a unit sounds like nothing — but to someone with loose change left at month's end, that's dignity.

items.sourceNote: 短暫外出關冷氣其實超虧 專家破解迷思 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
專家：「短暫外出別關冷氣，重開更耗電，超虧。」
我出門倒個垃圾，站在電梯口天人交戰：
關了，怕回來重開更貴；不關，怕鄰居以為我家在養企鵝。
最後我選擇：垃圾不倒了，回家吹冷氣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個為了「要不要關冷氣」可以在門口站三分鐘的選擇障礙朋友
variations: 省電最難的部分，從來不是技術，是判斷我這趟出門到底算不算「短暫」。
變頻冷氣的世界裡，連倒垃圾都是一場需要精算的金融決策。

en.content:
Expert: 'Don't switch off the AC for a quick errand — restarting it burns more power. Big loss.'
I step out to take the trash and freeze at the elevator, torn:
turn it off and risk a pricier restart, or leave it on and let the neighbors think I'm raising penguins?
Final call: skip the trash, go back inside, enjoy the AC.
en.editorNote: For the indecisive friend who stalls at the door for three minutes over whether to kill the AC
variations: The hardest part of saving energy was never the tech — it's deciding whether this errand counts as 'quick.'
In the world of inverter AC, even taking out the trash is a financial decision requiring spreadsheets.

items.sourceNote: 120 度以下不漲價 級距分界 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
懶人包：「一個月用 120 度以下，夏月電價不漲，跟平常一樣。」
我看著家裡那台 24 小時運轉的冷氣，輕輕地笑了。
120 度，是給住在別人家的人準備的數字。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個看到「120 度以內不漲」就燃起一絲希望、然後立刻熄滅的朋友
variations: 夏天用電 120 度以內的人，跟年薪百萬的人一樣——我知道你們存在，但我從沒見過。
台電的善意級距，是用來提醒你：原來真的有人這麼省。

en.content:
Guide: 'Stay under 120 kWh a month and summer rates don't go up — same as usual.'
I glance at the AC that's been running 24/7 and let out a soft little laugh.
120 kWh is a number designed for people who live in someone else's house.
en.editorNote: For the friend who feels a flicker of hope at 'under 120 kWh' before it instantly dies
variations: People who use under 120 kWh in summer are like millionaires — I know you exist, I've just never met one.
The gentle lower tier exists only to remind you that some people really are that frugal.

items.sourceNote: 定期清洗冷氣濾網 省電 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
省電第一招：每兩三週清一次冷氣濾網。
我上次清濾網，是去年的這個時候，也是看到這篇文章的時候。
所以對我來說，清濾網不是保養，是一種年度儀式，配合夏月電價一年舉辦一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每年只在收到第一張夏季帳單後才想起濾網存在的朋友
variations: 冷氣濾網跟健身房會員一樣：辦了、知道要用、然後一年想起一次。
我家冷氣濾網的灰塵，已經厚到可以單獨成立一個生態系。

en.content:
Energy tip #1: clean your AC filter every two to three weeks.
The last time I cleaned mine was this time last year — also the last time I read an article like this.
So for me, cleaning the filter isn't maintenance, it's an annual ritual, held once a year alongside the summer rate hike.
en.editorNote: For the friend who only remembers the filter exists after the first summer bill lands
variations: AC filters are like gym memberships: you sign up, you know you should use it, you remember once a year.
The dust on my AC filter is now thick enough to qualify as its own ecosystem.

items.sourceNote: 尖峰電價暴漲 3.5 倍 凌晨洗衣服省錢 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
專家：「尖峰時段電價暴漲，建議凌晨再洗衣服。」
於是我家現在的作息是：白天上班，晚上加班，凌晨兩點起來洗衣服。
為了省那幾塊錢，我把自己活成了一台 24 小時運轉的家電。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個真的設半夜鬧鐘起來洗衣服的省錢戰士，敬他的黑眼圈
variations: 離峰電價的真相：省下來的錢，剛好等於我熬夜買的眼霜。
凌晨兩點的洗衣機聲，是這個時代最勵志也最心酸的背景音樂。

en.content:
Expert: 'Peak rates are spiking, so do your laundry after midnight.'
So now my schedule goes: work all day, overtime all evening, wake up at 2 AM to run the washer.
To save a few bucks, I've turned myself into a 24/7 household appliance.
en.editorNote: Send to the savings warrior who actually sets a 2 AM alarm for laundry — salute the eye bags
variations: The truth about off-peak rates: what I save equals exactly what I spend on under-eye cream.
A washing machine running at 2 AM is the most inspiring and most depressing soundtrack of our era.

items.sourceNote: 夏月電價一般家庭增加 70% register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
新聞：「夏月電價一開跑，一般家庭電費平均增加 70%。」
我：「70% 耶，這如果是薪水該有多好。」
台電：「不，這只用在會讓你變窮的地方。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個看到「電費漲 70%」標題就先把冷氣關掉的同事，純屬本能反應
variations: 人生中所有的「漲 70%」，都精準地避開了我的存款。
夏月電價漲 70%，我的省電決心也漲 70%，可惜兩者都撐不過一個禮拜。

en.content:
News: 'Summer rates kick in, raising the average household bill by 70%.'
Me: 'Seventy percent — imagine if that were my salary.'
Power company: 'No. That number only applies to things that make you poorer.'
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who instinctively kills the AC the second they see a '70% hike' headline
variations: Every '70% increase' in my life has carefully avoided my savings account.
Summer rates rose 70%, my resolve to save power rose 70% — sadly neither survived the week.

items.sourceNote: PTT 大家怎麼省冷氣電費 太陽能 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
鄉民：「想根本解決電費，不如裝太陽能板。」
我：「裝太陽能板要先有屋頂。」
我：「有屋頂要先有房子。」
我：「有房子的人，根本不會在意這點電費。」
結論：能煩惱電費，本身就是一種沒有屋頂的浪漫。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被長輩建議「裝太陽能就好了啊」的無殼蝸牛朋友
variations: 解決電費的終極方法，從第一步就把我刷掉了。
太陽能板是有錢人的省電法，吹電風扇是我的。

en.content:
Internet: 'If you really want to fix your bill, just install solar panels.'
Me: 'Solar panels need a roof.'
Me: 'A roof needs a house.'
Me: 'And anyone with a house doesn't care about this bill.'
Conclusion: being able to worry about the electric bill is its own kind of roofless romance.
en.editorNote: For the renter friend whose relatives keep suggesting 'just get solar panels'
variations: The ultimate fix for the electric bill filters me out at step one.
Solar panels are how rich people save power. A pedestal fan is how I do.

items.sourceNote: 電價漲 冷氣爽吹 27 度抗漲 降溫神器 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
新聞教大家：「電價漲，冷氣設 27 度，再搭降溫神器抗漲。」
所謂的降溫神器，我研究了半天，最後發現就是——少穿一件、多流一點汗、然後說服自己 27 度其實很舒服。
抗漲成功的關鍵，原來是自我催眠。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個把冷氣設 27 度、然後一直跟你強調「其實沒有很熱」的朋友
variations: 對抗電價最強的神器，是你願意說服自己不熱的那顆心。
27 度抗漲法的精髓：身體覺得熱，但帳單覺得你成熟了。

en.content:
The news taught everyone: 'Rates are up — set the AC to 80°F and pair it with a cooling gadget to beat the hike.'
I researched what that magic gadget was for ages, and it turned out to be: wear one less layer, sweat a little more, and convince yourself 80°F is actually comfortable.
Turns out the key to beating the hike is self-hypnosis.
en.editorNote: For the friend who sets the AC to 80°F and keeps insisting to you that 'it's really not that hot'
variations: The strongest gadget against rising rates is your own willingness to believe you're not hot.
The essence of the 80°F method: your body feels the heat, but your bill thinks you've matured.

items.sourceNote: 鄉民「吹兩個月冷氣電費才 1205」炫耀文 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
有人發文：「我冷氣吹整整兩個月，電費才一千出頭，是不是很省？」
底下三百推都在問：你家到底是不開窗，還是根本不開冷氣，還是不住人。
結論：在電費版炫耀省錢，跟在減肥版炫耀吃不胖一樣欠揍。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每次都在群組貼低電費截圖、等大家拜託他教學的朋友
variations: 「我電費才一千」這種文，存在的唯一目的，就是讓我們其他人集體血壓升高。
電費低到可以發文炫耀，本身就是一種我這輩子達不到的成就。

en.content:
Someone posts: 'Ran my AC for two whole months and the bill was barely forty bucks. Pretty frugal, right?'
Three hundred replies all asking the same thing: do you not open the windows, not run the AC, or not actually live there?
Conclusion: bragging about a low bill in a power-saving forum is as punchable as bragging about eating anything without gaining weight.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps posting low-bill screenshots and waiting for everyone to beg for the secret
variations: The 'my bill was only forty bucks' post exists for one reason: to raise the collective blood pressure of the rest of us.
Having a bill low enough to brag about is, in itself, an achievement I'll never reach.

items.sourceNote: 四人一隻倉鼠 冷氣 24 小時 電費 1700 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我家四個人加一隻倉鼠，主臥冷氣幾乎 24 小時開。
月底帳單來，我盯著數字，轉頭看那隻在滾輪上跑步的倉鼠：
「全家最不該負擔這筆錢的，是你，但全家最怕熱的，也是你。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個夏天為了寵物不敢關冷氣、自己卻在客廳吹電風扇的朋友
variations: 夏天養寵物的真相：牠負責可愛，我負責付牠那份冷氣錢。
我家冷氣是為了倉鼠開的，我只是剛好住在同一個房間裡享福的人。

en.content:
Four people and one hamster in my place, master bedroom AC running nearly 24/7.
The bill arrives, I stare at the number, then turn to the hamster sprinting on its wheel:
'The one who should least have to pay for this is you — and the one who can least handle the heat is also you.'
en.editorNote: For the friend who won't kill the AC because of the pet, yet sits in the living room with a fan
variations: The truth about summer pets: they handle the cute, I handle their share of the AC bill.
The AC runs for the hamster. I just happen to live in the same room and benefit.

items.sourceNote: 「熱到電費寄警告簡訊」its-so-hot joke；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
今天熱到什麼程度？
熱到我手機跳出一封簡訊，不是天氣警報，是台電傳來的：
「您好，偵測到您本期用電異常飆升，請問您是否需要協助——心理上的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一收到電力公司任何通知就心驚膽跳的朋友，配一張手機鎖屏截圖
variations: 熱到我的電表開始用比我還快的速度數錢。
熱到連帳單都覺得不好意思，但還是照寄了。

en.content:
How hot was it today?
So hot my phone buzzed with a text — not a weather alert, but one from the power company:
'Hi there, we've detected an unusual spike in your usage. Do you require assistance — emotionally?'
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who flinches at any notification from the utility company — pair with a lock-screen screenshot
variations: So hot my electric meter started counting money faster than I ever could.
So hot even the bill felt a little embarrassed — but mailed itself anyway.

items.sourceNote: 「熱到溫度計情緒崩潰放棄」its-so-hot joke；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
今天熱到牆上那支溫度計，量到一半，情緒崩潰，直接放棄人生。
我跟它說：「沒事，我也是。」
然後我們兩個一起，在 36 度的房間裡，靜靜地融化。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合下午兩點熱到連抱怨都懶的時刻，配一張溫度計或溫度數字的照片
variations: 今天的溫度計不是壞了，是它看完數字後選擇逃避現實。
熱到溫度計都不想負責任了，那我又憑什麼振作。

en.content:
It was so hot today that the thermometer on my wall read halfway up, had an emotional breakdown, and just gave up on life.
I told it: 'It's okay. Same.'
Then the two of us quietly melted together in a 97-degree room.
en.editorNote: For that 2PM moment when you're too hot to even complain — pair with a photo of a thermometer or temperature reading
variations: The thermometer isn't broken — it just saw the number and chose denial.
If even the thermometer refuses to take responsibility, why should I pull myself together?

items.sourceNote: 「夏天電費比房租還高」summer bachelor AC quote；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
今年夏天我達成一個從沒想過的成就：
我的電費，比我的房租還高。
房東收的是這個家的使用權，台電收的是這個家能不能住人的決定權。
現在我才知道，誰才是真正的房東。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個某個月電費史詩級爆表、開始懷疑人生的租屋族朋友
variations: 當電費超過房租的那一刻，台電就正式變成我精神上的二房東。
我付房租買到一個空間，付電費才買到那個空間能活下去的權利。

en.content:
This summer I unlocked an achievement I never wanted:
my electric bill came in higher than my rent.
The landlord charges me for the right to use this place; the power company charges me for whether this place is livable at all.
Now I finally know who the real landlord is.
en.editorNote: For the renter friend whose bill went epically over budget one month and started questioning everything
variations: The moment the electric bill passes the rent, the power company officially becomes my spiritual subletter.
Rent buys me the space. The electric bill buys me the right to survive in it.

items.sourceNote: 2026 史上最貴電價 最高級距漲 26% 8.86 元；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
新聞：「今年夏月電價，最高級距創史上最貴，每度 8.86 元。」
我：「8.86 元一度，這個數字，有種我這輩子都到不了的高度。」
台電：「別擔心，你開冷氣開個兩個月，就到了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個看到「史上最貴電價」標題、手已經默默伸向冷氣遙控器的同事
variations: 今年夏天最勵志的事，是台電相信我有能力衝上最高級距。
8.86 元一度電，是台電給認真吹冷氣的人，頒發的成就獎章。

en.content:
News: 'This summer's top-tier rate is the most expensive on record.'
Me: 'That number feels like a height I'll never reach in my life.'
Power company: 'Don't worry. Run the AC for two months and you'll get there.'
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker whose hand drifts toward the AC remote the second they read 'most expensive rate ever'
variations: The most inspiring thing this summer is the power company believing I can climb to the top tier.
The record top-tier rate is the achievement badge they hand out to anyone who runs the AC seriously.

items.sourceNote: 住宅夏季平均每戶每月電費增加約 446 元；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
台電統計：「夏天每戶平均每月多繳 446 元。」
446 元，聽起來像是可以好好吃一頓的數字。
結果它不是飯，它是空氣——是我家那台冷氣，每個月吐出來的那一口涼。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個算過「這筆電費能吃幾頓好料」、然後默默把外送 App 關掉的朋友
variations: 夏天多繳的那 446 元，是我跟夏天和解的入場費。
446 元買不到一頓大餐，只買得到一個月不會中暑的權利。

en.content:
Stats: 'The average household pays about 446 dollars more a month in summer.'
That number sounds like enough for a proper nice dinner.
Turns out it's not food. It's air — the breath of cool my AC exhales every single month.
en.editorNote: For the friend who calculated how many nice meals that bill could buy, then quietly closed the delivery app
variations: The extra I pay each summer is the entry fee for making peace with the season.
It won't buy a feast — only the right to go a whole month without heatstroke.

items.sourceNote: 冬天可以加衣 夏天脫光也沒用 meme；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
冬天冷，至少還能一件一件加，加到暖為止，免費。
夏天熱，你脫到剩一條內褲，還是熱，而且這時候唯一的解法，要錢。
結論：老天爺對冬天比較仁慈，對夏天只想跟你收費。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個夏天在家已經穿到最少、還是熱到懷疑人生的朋友
variations: 冬天的溫暖可以靠棉被，夏天的涼爽只能靠帳單。
對抗寒冷，人類有衣服；對抗炎熱，人類只有台電。

en.content:
In winter you can add layers, one by one, until you're warm — for free.
In summer you can strip down to your last shred of clothing and you're still hot, and the only fix left costs money.
Conclusion: the universe is merciful to winter and just wants to bill you for summer.
en.editorNote: For the friend who's already wearing the bare minimum at home and is still questioning their life choices
variations: Winter warmth comes from a blanket. Summer coolness comes from a bill.
To fight the cold, humans have clothes. To fight the heat, humans have the power company.

items.sourceNote: 台電揭帳單變貴真相 用電量比費率影響更大；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
台電解釋：「帳單變貴，其實費率只佔一部分，真正的關鍵是你用得太多。」
我：「所以你的意思是，這不是你的錯，是我的錯。」
台電：「你終於懂了。」
我：「那我可以退貨重來這個夏天嗎。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每次看完台電澄清文、反而更想關冷氣的朋友
variations: 台電最會的，不是發電，是讓你心甘情願地覺得電費貴是你的責任。
原來電費貴的真相不是漲價，是我太愛活著、太想涼快。

en.content:
The power company explains: 'Your bill went up, but the rate is only part of it — the real key is you're using too much.'
Me: 'So what you're saying is, it's not your fault, it's mine.'
Power company: 'Now you get it.'
Me: 'Can I return this summer and start over?'
en.editorNote: For the friend who reads the utility's clarification post and somehow wants to kill the AC even more
variations: The power company's real talent isn't generating electricity — it's making you genuinely feel the bill is your own fault.
Turns out the truth behind a high bill isn't the rate hike. It's that I love being alive and cool too much.

items.sourceNote: 離峰時段用電比較省 半夜用電；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
懶人包：「想省錢，把高耗電活動移到離峰時段，半夜用電最便宜。」
於是我現在最幸福的時刻，是凌晨一點，全世界都睡了，只有我跟冷氣醒著。
白天的涼是有罪惡感的，半夜的涼，才是台電允許我擁有的那一份。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個白天捨不得開冷氣、專門等半夜離峰才大開的夜貓子朋友
variations: 離峰電價把我改造成一隻晝伏夜出、靠便宜時段續命的生物。
夏天最便宜的涼快，藏在所有人都睡著的那幾個小時裡。

en.content:
Guide: 'To save money, shift high-power activities to off-peak hours — electricity is cheapest after midnight.'
So now my happiest moment is 1 AM, when the whole world is asleep and only the AC and I are awake.
Daytime cool comes with guilt. The cool after midnight is the share the power company actually lets me have.
en.editorNote: For the night-owl friend who can't bear to run the AC by day and waits for cheap off-peak hours to blast it
variations: Off-peak rates have turned me into a creature that sleeps by day and survives on the cheap hours.
Summer's cheapest coolness hides in the few hours when everyone else is asleep.

items.sourceNote: PTT 定頻 vs 變頻冷氣 電費差異討論；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
鄉民勸我：「快把家裡那台十年定頻冷氣換掉，變頻省超多。」
我：「換一台變頻要三萬。」
我：「省下來的電費，要省七年才回本。」
我：「但我的舊冷氣，可能撐不到那一天，我也是。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個家裡還在用老定頻冷氣、每年都在「換或不換」之間天人交戰的朋友
variations: 變頻冷氣最省的不是電，是你說服自己「這是投資」時的那份心安。
省電的盡頭是換新機，換新機的盡頭是先有三萬。

en.content:
Internet advice: 'Ditch that ten-year-old fixed-speed AC, an inverter unit saves a ton.'
Me: 'A new inverter costs a thousand bucks.'
Me: 'The energy savings take seven years to break even.'
Me: 'But my old AC probably won't last that long. Neither will I.'
en.editorNote: For the friend still running an ancient fixed-speed unit, agonizing every year over whether to replace it
variations: The thing an inverter AC saves most isn't electricity — it's the peace of mind from calling it an 'investment.'
The end of saving power is buying a new unit. The end of buying a new unit is first having a thousand bucks.

items.sourceNote: 辦公室一半人愛冷氣一半人冷到失溫 meme；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
辦公室的冷氣溫度，是一個永遠無法達成的和平協議。
靠窗那排：「太熱，調低一點。」
靠冷氣那排：「我已經披兩件外套了。」
中間那個調溫度的人：每天默默承受全公司的恨意，像個沒人感謝的聯合國。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個在公司負責管冷氣遙控器、兩面不是人的同事
variations: 辦公室冷氣的溫度，是一個沒有正確答案、但每個人都有意見的數字。
夏天最危險的職位，是辦公室那個能碰冷氣遙控器的人。

en.content:
The office thermostat is a peace treaty that can never be signed.
Window row: 'Too hot, turn it down.'
Vent row: 'I'm already wearing two jackets.'
The one person allowed to touch the controls: silently absorbing the hatred of the entire floor, like a UN nobody thanks.
en.editorNote: For the coworker stuck managing the office AC remote, hated by both factions
variations: The office temperature is a number with no right answer that everyone has an opinion about.
The most dangerous job in summer is being the one person allowed to touch the office thermostat.

items.sourceNote: 冷氣搭循環扇 設定 26-28 度 省電建議；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
商週教我：「冷氣設 26 到 28 度，搭一台循環扇，把冷空氣推勻，壓縮機就不用一直全力跑。」
我認真照做，結果發現最被推勻的，是我對省錢的執念——
它現在均勻地分布在我房間的每一個角落，跟冷氣的涼一樣，看不見，但摸得到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個真的去買了循環扇、然後開始研究最佳擺位的省電強迫症朋友
variations: 循環扇的功能，是把冷空氣推勻，順便把我省錢的焦慮也推勻。
省電的最高境界，是讓壓縮機休息，讓自己的腦袋全力運轉。

en.content:
The guide taught me: 'Set the AC to 78-82°F, add an air circulator to spread the cool air evenly, and the compressor won't have to run at full blast.'
I followed it faithfully, and discovered the thing most evenly spread was my obsession with saving money —
it now sits in every corner of my room, just like the cool air: invisible, but you can feel it.
en.editorNote: For the friend who actually bought a circulator and started obsessing over its optimal placement
variations: The circulator spreads the cool air evenly, and my money anxiety right along with it.
The highest form of saving power is letting the compressor rest while your brain runs at full blast.

items.sourceNote: 沒冷氣只有電風扇的夏天 hot weather meme；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
沒裝冷氣的夏天，是這樣過的：
電風扇對著我吹，吹出來的是熱風；
我對著電風扇看，看到的是它跟我一樣在拼命，卻一樣無能為力。
我們兩個對望，像兩個被生活逼到牆角、還假裝沒事的成年人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個房間沒裝冷氣、整個夏天靠一台電風扇硬撐的朋友
variations: 電風扇在夏天的功能，是把熱空氣搬來搬去，順便陪你一起熱。
沒冷氣的夏天教會我一件事：原來風，也可以是熱的。

en.content:
A summer with no AC goes like this:
the fan blows at me, and what comes out is hot air;
I stare back at the fan and see something working just as hard as I am, and just as powerless.
We lock eyes, two adults backed into a corner by life, both pretending we're fine.
en.editorNote: For the friend with no AC in their room, white-knuckling the whole summer on a single fan
variations: A fan's job in summer is to move hot air around and keep you company while you sweat.
A summer with no AC taught me one thing: turns out wind can be hot too.

items.sourceNote: 台電揭真相 冷氣 24 小時運轉才是帳單暴漲主因 非費率；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
台電澄清：「帳單暴漲的真兇，不是漲價，是你那台 24 小時沒關過的冷氣。」
我看著那台從六月一號開到現在沒熄過火的冷氣，輕聲對它說：
「他們在說你。」
冷氣沒回應，繼續忠心地涼著，像個明知會被怪罪、還是選擇留下來的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個冷氣從入夏就沒關過、看到台電澄清文卻捨不得責怪冷氣的朋友
variations: 帳單暴漲的兇手，台電查到最後，發現是那個最捨不得熱到自己的我。
我家冷氣 24 小時運轉的真相是：不是它停不下來，是我不准它停。

en.content:
The power company clarifies: 'The real culprit behind the spike isn't the rate hike — it's that AC of yours that hasn't switched off in days.'
I look at the unit that hasn't gone cold since June 1st and say softly:
'They're talking about you.'
The AC doesn't answer, just keeps loyally cooling — like someone who knows they'll get blamed and chooses to stay anyway.
en.editorNote: For the friend whose AC hasn't stopped since summer began, who reads the utility's clarification but can't bring themselves to blame it
variations: The power company traced the spike to its source and found it was me — the one who couldn't bear to let myself get hot.
The truth about my 24/7 AC: it's not that it can't stop. It's that I won't let it.

items.sourceNote: 查核：網傳夏月三段式電價最高一度 8.12 元為不實資訊；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
家族群組瘋傳：「夏月電價一度飆到 8 塊多，快關冷氣！」
查核網站：「假的，家庭用電還是看累進級距。」
我：「所以是真的會變貴，只是沒那麼戲劇化。」
結論：唯一不假的，是我每年看到這則謠言時，那股被嚇到的真心。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每年都把「電價飆 8 塊」謠言轉進家族群組的長輩，附上查核連結
variations: 電費謠言年年新，唯一不變的是我看到數字就先把冷氣關掉的反射動作。
夏天的恐怖故事不用編，台電的真實級距就夠嚇人了。

en.content:
Family chat, going viral: 'Summer rates just spiked past eight bucks a unit — kill the AC now!'
Fact-check site: 'False. Household bills still follow the tiered scale.'
Me: 'So it does get pricier, just less dramatically.'
Conclusion: the only thing that isn't fake is how genuinely scared I am every year this rumor resurfaces.
en.editorNote: Send to the relative who forwards the 'rates spiked to eight bucks' rumor every year — attach the fact-check link
variations: The electric-bill rumors change every year; the only constant is my reflex to kill the AC the second I see a number.
You don't need to invent summer horror stories — the real tiered rates are scary enough.

items.sourceNote: 夏季電費試算 鄉民研究累進級距 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
六月開始，我學會了一個全新技能：用台電試算頁面，預估我這期帳單。
我把每天開幾小時冷氣輸進去，按下計算。
螢幕跳出數字的那一刻，我懂了——
有些答案，不知道反而比較幸福。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個第一次認真用電費試算機、然後馬上後悔的朋友
variations: 電費試算機是夏天唯一一個你會希望它算錯的計算機。
我預估了這期電費，現在我比帳單還早一步崩潰。

en.content:
Starting in June, I learned a brand-new skill: using the utility's calculator to forecast my bill.
I type in how many hours of AC I run each day and hit calculate.
The second the number appears on screen, I understand —
some answers leave you happier not knowing.
en.editorNote: For the friend who seriously used the bill calculator once and immediately regretted it
variations: The bill estimator is the only calculator in summer you actually hope gets it wrong.
I forecast this cycle's bill, and now I get to have my breakdown one step ahead of the envelope.

items.sourceNote: too hot to function hot weather meme；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
夏天的我，分成兩種狀態：
在冷氣房裡：勉強算個正常運作的人類。
離開冷氣房三分鐘：一灘有意識、會冒汗、偶爾還能講話的液體。
所以我不是不想出門，我是怕我撐不到目的地就先蒸發。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一到夏天就拒絕所有戶外邀約、理由都是「太熱」的朋友
variations: 夏天的我只有兩種型態：開冷氣的人，跟關冷氣的一灘水。
別約我中午見面，那個時段的我，已經不是固體了。

en.content:
In summer I exist in two states:
Inside the AC: a barely functioning human being.
Three minutes outside the AC: a conscious, sweating, occasionally verbal puddle.
So it's not that I don't want to go out — I'm afraid I'll evaporate before I reach the destination.
en.editorNote: For the friend who declines every outdoor invite all summer with the same reason: 'too hot'
variations: I have two summer forms: a person with AC, and a puddle without it.
Don't ask to meet at noon — by that hour, I'm no longer a solid.

items.sourceNote: 台電認證 變頻冷氣短暫外出不關更省 但長時間要關 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
台電認證：「變頻冷氣短暫外出別關，但離開超過一小時就該關。」
於是我每次出門前，都要先預測未來：
「我這趟，到底會不會超過一小時？」
我連自己晚餐吃什麼都決定不了，現在還要替一台冷氣做時間管理。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個出門前要站在冷氣前面盤算「我多久會回來」的選擇障礙朋友
variations: 省電的前提，是你得先成為一個能準確預測自己行程的人——我做不到。
變頻冷氣最難的不是設定，是它逼我承認我根本不知道自己幾點回家。

en.content:
The power company confirms: 'Don't switch off inverter AC for a quick errand, but turn it off if you'll be gone over an hour.'
So now, before every trip out, I have to predict the future:
'Will this errand take more than an hour?'
I can't even decide what to have for dinner, and now I'm doing time management for an air conditioner.
en.editorNote: For the indecisive friend who stands in front of the AC calculating 'how long will I be gone' before every trip
variations: Saving power assumes you can accurately predict your own schedule — I cannot.
The hardest part of inverter AC isn't the settings; it's being forced to admit I have no idea when I'll be home.

items.sourceNote: 2026 夏季電費 6/1-9/30 四個月 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
夏月電價從六月一號開始，九月三十號結束。
別人過夏天是在數還剩幾天放假，
我是在數還剩幾天，台電才肯放過我的錢包。
四個月，一百二十二天，我跟我的存款，一起撐。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在六月初發文，配一張月曆截圖，標出九月三十號那格畫圈
variations: 我的夏天不是用節氣算的，是用台電的夏月電價區間算的。
九月三十號之後，吹冷氣才會重新變回一件單純的事。

en.content:
Summer rates run from June 1st to September 30th.
Other people spend summer counting down the days of vacation left.
I spend it counting down the days until the power company finally lets my wallet go.
Four months, 122 days, me and my savings, holding the line together.
en.editorNote: Post in early June with a calendar screenshot and September 30th circled
variations: My summer isn't measured by the solstice — it's measured by the utility's peak-rate window.
After September 30th, running the AC goes back to being a simple thing again.

items.sourceNote: NPR 報導 AI 資料中心推升電價 一般家庭夏天負擔；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
新聞說：「AI 資料中心愈蓋愈多，整個電網的電都變貴了。」
所以我家電費飆高，原因之一是地球另一端有一台超級電腦，正在很努力地幫某個人想晚餐要吃什麼。
結論：我吹冷氣付的錢，有一部分，是替全人類的 AI 繳的冷氣費。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一邊問 AI 怎麼省電、一邊發現 AI 正是電費變貴元兇的朋友
variations: 我的電費裡，有一條看不見的帳目，叫做「替全世界的伺服器降溫」。
AI 愈聰明，我家電費就愈高，這大概是這個時代最公平的稅。

en.content:
The news says: 'As AI data centers multiply, electricity across the whole grid is getting pricier.'
So one reason my bill spiked is that somewhere on the other side of the planet, a supercomputer is working hard to help someone decide what to have for dinner.
Conclusion: part of what I pay to run my AC is actually covering the cooling bill for all of humanity's AI.
en.editorNote: For the friend who asks AI how to save power, then realizes AI is the very reason power got expensive
variations: There's an invisible line item in my bill called 'cooling the world's servers.'
The smarter AI gets, the higher my bill climbs — probably the fairest tax of our era.

items.sourceNote: KPBS/NPR 報導 有人靠捐血漿付電費 夏季帳單破 300 美元；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
看到新聞說有人為了付夏天的電費，每週去捐兩次血漿。
我愣了一下，然後默默看向自己的手臂，認真評估了一下。
夏天的台灣教會我一件事：原來「身上有什麼可以換錢」，是一個會在拆帳單那天突然浮現的問題。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個帳單來的那天會開始盤點家裡有什麼能賣的朋友，苦中作樂
variations: 夏天的電費，逼我開始研究我這個人除了會喊熱，還剩下什麼價值。
為了冷氣費，我願意付出的東西，正在以一種我不太想承認的速度增加。

en.content:
I read that someone donates plasma twice a week just to cover their summer electric bill.
I paused, glanced down at my own arm, and gave it a genuinely serious assessment.
Summer taught me one thing: 'what do I have that I could trade for cash' is a question that surfaces out of nowhere the day the bill arrives.
en.editorNote: For the friend who starts mentally inventorying what's sellable in the house the day the bill lands
variations: The summer bill forced me to figure out what I'm worth besides being good at complaining about the heat.
The list of things I'd give up to afford the AC is growing at a speed I'd rather not admit.

items.sourceNote: PTT 父母看到帳單飆升後寧願不開冷氣 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我爸媽以前的原則是：「冷氣會涼就好，多少錢都沒關係。」
直到他們看見第一張夏月帳單。
現在他們的新原則是：「再熱都撐得過去，命是自己的，錢是台電的。」
我回家發現他們坐在 33 度的客廳搧扇子，那一刻我懂了，省電是會遺傳的，但要先被嚇過一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個夏天回老家、發現爸媽寧願熱也不開冷氣的朋友，又好氣又心疼
variations: 我爸媽對冷氣的態度，在收到帳單前後，是兩個完全不同的人。
長大後最心酸的畫面，是爸媽捨不得開冷氣，卻一直叫我回房間吹。

en.content:
My parents' old rule: 'As long as the AC keeps us cool, the cost doesn't matter.'
Then they saw the first summer bill.
Their new rule: 'We can survive any amount of heat — our lives are our own, but the money belongs to the power company.'
I came home to find them fanning themselves in a 91-degree living room, and that's when I understood: frugality is hereditary, but only after one good scare.
en.editorNote: For the friend who goes home in summer to find their parents toughing out the heat rather than running the AC — equal parts exasperated and heartbroken
variations: My parents before and after the bill are two completely different people about the AC.
The most bittersweet sight of growing up is parents who won't run the AC for themselves but keep telling me to go cool off in my room.

items.sourceNote: NPR 報導 反常高溫 冷氣風扇超時運轉 多買度數；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
今年夏天熱到反常，冷氣跟電風扇全部超時加班。
以前我覺得「電費貴」是台電的問題，現在我知道，是天氣先動的手，台電只是負責收尾。
我跟我的冷氣，今年都在做一份沒有上限、沒有加班費、還要倒貼的工作。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個今年熱到開始懷疑是不是地球壞掉的朋友，配一張溫度破紀錄的截圖
variations: 今年的熱，是那種會讓你的冷氣跟你的存款一起喊累的熱。
天氣負責讓我熱，台電負責讓我窮，分工明確，配合無間。

en.content:
This summer is abnormally hot, and the AC and fans are all pulling overtime.
I used to think 'expensive electricity' was the power company's doing. Now I know the weather strikes first, and the power company just handles the cleanup.
This year, my AC and I are both working a job with no ceiling, no overtime pay, and an out-of-pocket cost.
en.editorNote: For the friend whose heat this year has them wondering if the planet is broken — pair with a record-high temperature screenshot
variations: This year's heat is the kind that leaves your AC and your savings both gasping for breath.
The weather makes me hot, the power company makes me broke — clear division of labor, seamless teamwork.

items.sourceNote: 夏月電價上路 冷氣開關之間的猶豫 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
夏天的我，手指停在冷氣開關上的時間，比真正吹到冷氣的時間還長。
開，怕帳單；不開，怕中暑。
我就這樣維持著一個微妙的平衡——熱到剛好快受不了，但還沒受不了的那個瞬間，我管它叫「省電的甜蜜點」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每次開冷氣前都要先天人交戰三秒鐘的朋友，他一定懂這個甜蜜點
variations: 夏天最難的不是熱，是決定到底要不要按下那顆開關。
我跟冷氣之間，隔著一整個夏天的猶豫。

en.content:
In summer, my finger hovers over the AC switch longer than I actually spend in the cold air.
Turn it on, fear the bill; leave it off, fear heatstroke.
So I maintain a delicate balance — hot enough to be right on the edge of unbearable, but not quite there. I call that the 'energy-saving sweet spot.'
en.editorNote: For the friend who agonizes for three full seconds before turning on the AC every single time — they know this sweet spot well
variations: The hardest part of summer isn't the heat — it's deciding whether to press that switch.
Between me and the AC stands an entire summer's worth of hesitation.

items.sourceNote: 120 度以下維持 1.78 元不漲 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
新聞說：「一個月用 120 度以內的人，夏天電價維持 1.78 元，完全不漲。」
我認真盤算了一下：要壓在 120 度，我大概只能開冰箱，然後人住進冰箱旁邊。
所謂「不漲的善意」，是台電對著一群開冷氣的人，溫柔地說一句我們永遠用不到的好話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每年都認真研究級距、然後發現自己永遠在 120 度上面的朋友
variations: 1.78 元那一格，是電價表上最美、也最不關我的事的風景。
夏天能用不到 120 度的人，跟傳說中的生物一樣，存在於資料裡，但沒人見過。

en.content:
The news says: 'If you stay under 120 kWh a month, your summer rate holds at the base price — no increase at all.'
I did the math: to stay under 120, I'd basically have to open the fridge and move in next to it.
That 'no-increase kindness' is the power company gently telling a room full of AC users something none of us will ever get to use.
en.editorNote: For the friend who studies the tiers every year only to realize they live permanently above 120 kWh
variations: The lowest tier is the prettiest line on the rate chart, and the one least relevant to my life.
People who use under 120 kWh in summer are like cryptids: documented, but never actually witnessed.

items.sourceNote: electricity pun 'shocking' bill 一語雙關；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
朋友問我夏天電費怎麼樣，我說：很有電的感覺。
他說怎麼講？
我說：每次拆開帳單，我都會被電到，全身一震，然後手麻、心痛、腦袋一片空白。
台電不只供電，還順便附贈一次免費的觸電體驗。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個喜歡冷笑話、也剛收到夏季帳單的朋友，雙重打擊
variations: 夏天的電費單，是全台灣最有「電」的一張紙。
別人被愛情電到，我被台電電到，論強度，台電完勝。

en.content:
A friend asked how my summer electric bill is going. I said: shocking, honestly.
He asked what I meant.
I said: every time I open it, I get a jolt — full-body shock, numb hands, aching chest, blank mind.
The power company doesn't just deliver electricity. It throws in a free electrocution experience.
en.editorNote: Send to the pun-loving friend who also just got their summer bill — double impact
variations: The summer electric bill is the single most electrifying piece of paper in the country.
Other people get shocked by love. I get shocked by the utility company — and in terms of voltage, the utility wins.

items.sourceNote: 手搖飲消暑 vs 冷氣 成本對照 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
為了不開冷氣，我改用喝冰手搖消暑。
一杯 65，一天三杯，一個月下來——
我算了算，這筆錢拿去開冷氣，可以從早涼到晚，還有找。
原來最貴的省電法，是用嘴巴降溫。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個堅持不開冷氣、卻每天兩杯全糖去冰的朋友，幫他算清楚這筆帳
variations: 省下的冷氣錢，最後全部變成手搖飲的封膜，躺在我的回收桶裡。
夏天最諷刺的事：為了省冷氣錢喝的飲料，比冷氣還貴。

en.content:
To avoid running the AC, I switched to cooling down with iced bubble tea.
One cup is two bucks, three cups a day, and over a month —
I ran the numbers: that money could've run the AC from dawn to midnight, with change to spare.
Turns out the priciest way to save power is cooling yourself with your mouth.
en.editorNote: For the friend who refuses the AC but downs two full-sugar drinks a day — help them do the math
variations: The money I saved on AC ended up as a pile of bubble tea lids in my recycling bin.
Summer's greatest irony: the drinks I buy to skip the AC cost more than the AC.

items.sourceNote: 睡前電風扇取代冷氣 半夜熱醒 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我發誓今晚只開電風扇睡覺，為了省那一格電費。
前半夜：我是個有原則、會理財的成熟大人。
後半夜兩點：我滿身大汗坐起來，認清自己其實是個怕熱的肉體，伸手把冷氣按開。
省電的決心，撐不過一個熱夜。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每天睡前都立志只吹電風扇、結果半夜投降的朋友
variations: 電風扇是睡前的理想，冷氣是半夜的現實。
我的省電計畫，總是死在凌晨兩點那身汗裡。

en.content:
I swore I'd sleep with just the fan tonight, to save that one tier on the bill.
First half of the night: I'm a principled, financially responsible adult.
2 AM: I sit up drenched in sweat, accept that I'm really just a heat-sensitive body, and reach over to switch the AC on.
My resolve to save power can't survive a single hot night.
en.editorNote: For the friend who vows to use only the fan every night and surrenders by 2 AM
variations: The fan is the bedtime ideal. The AC is the 2 AM reality.
My energy-saving plan always dies in that 2 AM layer of sweat.

items.sourceNote: 電費帳單像恐怖片 envelope dread meme；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
如果夏天的電費單是一部電影，它一定是恐怖片。
劇情緩慢，氣氛壓抑，你早就知道結局不會好，
但你還是會在拆開信封的那一刻，被那串數字嚇得倒抽一口氣。
而且最可怕的是——它每兩個月就出一部續集，永遠不會完結。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每次拆帳單都像在看鬼片、要從指縫偷看的朋友
variations: 夏天的電費單，是唯一一部我明知道結局還是會被嚇到的恐怖片。
電費單的恐怖之處，在於它不靠音效，光靠數字就能讓你心跳停一拍。

en.content:
If the summer electric bill were a movie, it'd be a horror film.
Slow pacing, oppressive mood, you already know the ending won't be good —
yet the moment you tear open the envelope, that string of numbers still makes you gasp.
And the scariest part: it drops a new sequel every two months and never wraps up.
en.editorNote: For the friend who opens every bill like a horror movie, peeking through their fingers
variations: The summer bill is the only horror film whose ending I know and still get scared by.
The terror of the electric bill needs no sound effects — the numbers alone skip a heartbeat.

items.sourceNote: 台電公布冷氣 26-28 度最省 一堆人吹錯 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
台電公布冷氣最省又舒適的溫度是 26 到 28 度，新聞標題說「一堆人都吹錯」。
我看完默默把冷氣調到 26 度，撐了五分鐘，又調回 24 度。
原來我不是吹錯，我是知道正確答案，但選擇了自己的快樂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每次看到「正確冷氣溫度」報導都會默默調溫、然後三分鐘後調回去的朋友
variations: 26 度是台電的標準答案，24 度是我的真心話。
知道正確溫度，跟願意忍受正確溫度，是兩個完全不同的境界。

en.content:
The power company announced the most efficient yet comfortable AC setting is 79-82°F, and the headline read 'tons of people are doing it wrong.'
I read it, quietly bumped the AC up to 79°F, lasted five minutes, then dropped it back to 75°F.
Turns out I'm not doing it wrong. I know the right answer — I just chose my own happiness.
en.editorNote: For the friend who nudges the thermostat up every time they read the 'correct AC temp' article, then caves three minutes later
variations: 79°F is the power company's official answer. 75°F is my honest truth.
Knowing the right temperature and being willing to endure it are two completely different levels of enlightenment.

items.sourceNote: 2026 節電獎勵取消保底 84 元 改實際度數計算 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
以前台電的節電獎勵，只要有省一點，每期保底發你 84 元。
今年改了，沒有保底，完全看你實際省幾度。
所以連那 84 元的安慰獎都被收回去了——
台電的意思是：你想領錢，請先證明你真的痛過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每期都在等那 84 元保底、今年發現被取消的省電老手
variations: 連 84 元的參加獎都沒了，今年的省電是純粹的精神修行。
台電取消保底獎勵的那一刻，我才懂什麼叫做「努力不一定有回報」。

en.content:
The power company used to hand you a guaranteed rebate every billing cycle just for saving a little.
This year they changed it: no floor, it all depends on the units you actually cut.
So even that little consolation prize got taken back —
what they're really saying is: want the money? First prove you genuinely suffered.
en.editorNote: For the seasoned saver who counted on the guaranteed rebate every cycle and just found out it's gone
variations: Even the participation trophy is gone — this year's saving is pure spiritual training.
The moment they killed the guaranteed rebate, I finally understood 'effort doesn't always pay off.'

items.sourceNote: stepping out of AC building summer temperature shock meme；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
在冷氣房待一整天後走出大樓的那一秒，
外面的熱浪像一巴掌打在臉上，
我的身體瞬間明白了一件事：剛剛那八小時的涼爽，原來是有人在付錢的。
而那個人，回家以後就是我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每天下班走出公司、被熱浪迎面打到的同事，配一張大樓出口的照片
variations: 夏天最現實的瞬間，是踏出冷氣大樓、想起家裡冷氣要自己付的那一刻。
冷氣房的門，是免費涼爽跟付費涼爽之間的分界線。

en.content:
The second you step out of an air-conditioned building after a full day inside,
the heat hits you like a slap across the face,
and your body instantly understands one thing: that eight hours of cool air? Somebody was paying for it.
And that somebody, once you get home, is you.
en.editorNote: For the coworker who gets smacked by a heat wave the moment they leave the office — pair with a photo of the building exit
variations: The most sobering summer moment is stepping out of an AC building and remembering the AC at home is on your dime.
The door of an air-conditioned building is the border between free cool air and the kind you pay for.

items.sourceNote: 變頻冷氣關機時機才是省電關鍵 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
研究了半天我才終於懂：變頻冷氣省不省電，重點根本不是「要不要關」，而是「什麼時候關」。
問題是，我連自己什麼時候該下班、什麼時候該睡覺都決定不了，
你現在要我精準判斷冷氣的最佳關機時機？
台電，你太看得起我的人生掌控力了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個認真研究變頻冷氣關機時機、結果發現自己根本無法執行的朋友
variations: 變頻冷氣的省電關鍵是關機時機，而我的人生關鍵，是連時機是什麼都搞不清楚。
我可以背出冷氣最佳關機時機，但我做不到——就像我知道幾點該睡，但從來沒做到。

en.content:
After ages of research I finally got it: whether an inverter AC saves money isn't about 'should I turn it off,' it's about 'when do I turn it off.'
Problem is, I can't even decide when to clock out or when to go to bed,
and now you want me to precisely judge the optimal moment to switch off the AC?
Power company, you vastly overestimate how much control I have over my own life.
en.editorNote: For the friend who studied inverter-AC timing seriously, then realized they can't actually execute any of it
variations: The key to saving with an inverter AC is timing — and the key to my life is having no idea what timing even is.
I can recite the optimal AC shut-off moment, but I can't do it — same way I know my bedtime and never hit it.

items.sourceNote: HVAC tech 夏天每個客戶都很急 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
夏天打去叫冷氣維修，每個人開頭都一樣：「師傅，可以今天來嗎？很急。」
師傅的行事曆裡，現在排了三百個「很急」。
所以排隊的順序，不是看誰比較急，是看誰比較會拜託、誰住得比較順路。
夏天的冷氣師傅，是全台灣最有權力決定你今晚能不能睡的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個冷氣壞掉、打了五家維修都排到下禮拜的苦主，請他先撐住
variations: 夏天的冷氣維修師傅，行程比醫生還滿，態度比醫生還難約。
「師傅可以今天來嗎」是夏天最常見、也最沒有用的一句開場白。

en.content:
Call for AC repair in summer and everyone opens the exact same way: 'Hey, can you come today? It's urgent.'
Right now the technician's calendar holds three hundred 'urgents.'
So the order isn't about who's most desperate — it's about who begs best and who lives on the way to the next job.
A summer AC technician is the single most powerful person in the country deciding whether you sleep tonight.
en.editorNote: For the friend whose AC died and got booked out to next week by five different shops — tell them to hang in there
variations: A summer AC tech's schedule is fuller than a doctor's and harder to book than one too.
'Can you come today?' is summer's most common — and most useless — opening line.

items.sourceNote: 2026 夏季電價上路 每月平均多繳 446 元 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
新聞：「夏季電價上路，平均每戶每月多繳 446 元。」
446 元，剛好是我一個禮拜的便當錢。
所以這個夏天，我等於是用兩個月、八個便當的代價，換一個不會在睡覺時融化的權利。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個看到「多繳 446 元」就開始換算成幾餐飯的精算型朋友
variations: 446 元的單位，從今天起正式從「錢」改成「八個便當」。
台電說多 446，我聽成「請你這個夏天少吃八餐」。

en.content:
News: 'Summer rates are here — the average household pays about 446 NT more per month.'
That's roughly a week of lunch money for me.
So this summer, I'm trading two months and eight lunches for the right not to melt in my sleep.
en.editorNote: For the friend who instantly converts '446 NT more' into how many meals it costs
variations: As of today, the unit for 446 NT is no longer 'money' — it's 'eight lunches.'
They said 446 more. I heard 'please skip eight meals this summer.'

items.sourceNote: 2026 電價凍漲 平均每度 3.7823 元 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
好消息：「今年電價凍漲，平均每度維持 3.7823 元。」
我看著新聞點點頭，然後看了一眼自己的用電度數，默默把「凍漲」兩個字劃掉。
電價沒漲，但我用的度數自己會漲，這才是真正的高手。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個聽到「凍漲」鬆一口氣、結果帳單還是創新高的朋友
variations: 電價凍漲，不代表帳單凍漲——這之間的差距，叫做冷氣。
單價沒動，動的是我，是我這顆 24 小時想吹冷氣的心。

en.content:
Good news: 'Rates are frozen this year, averaging 3.7823 NT per unit.'
I nod at the headline, glance at my own usage count, and quietly cross out the word 'frozen.'
The price didn't rise, but my kilowatt-hours rise all on their own — now that's true talent.
en.editorNote: For the friend who sighs with relief at 'frozen rates' and still gets a record bill
variations: Frozen rates don't mean a frozen bill — the gap between them is called air conditioning.
The price per unit didn't move. What moved was me, and my heart that wants AC 24/7.

items.sourceNote: 夏季離峰時段用電比較省 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
為了省電，我認真研究了離峰時段，發現最便宜的用電時間是凌晨到清晨。
於是我規劃了完美的省電人生：白天不在家、半夜才開冷氣、用電全部挪到離峰。
唯一的問題是——那段最便宜的時間，我都在睡覺，根本沒在用電。
我終於成為一個全年最省電的人，靠的是不在家。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個研究離峰電價、最後發現自己最省電的方法是出門上班的朋友
variations: 離峰電價教我的事：最省電的生活，就是不要待在自己家。
我把所有用電挪到離峰，才發現省最多的那個時段，叫做「沒在用」。

en.content:
To save money, I seriously studied off-peak hours and found the cheapest electricity runs from late night to dawn.
So I designed the perfect frugal life: gone all day, AC only after midnight, all my usage shifted off-peak.
The only problem — during that cheapest window, I'm asleep, not using anything.
I finally became the most energy-efficient person alive, purely by not being home.
en.editorNote: For the friend who studied off-peak rates and concluded their best saving tip is going to work
variations: What off-peak rates taught me: the most efficient lifestyle is simply not being in your own home.
I shifted all my usage off-peak, then realized the cheapest hours are the ones I spend asleep using nothing.

items.sourceNote: AC running all summer wallet empty HVAC meme 2026 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我的冷氣跟我的錢包，是反比關係：
冷氣愈涼，錢包愈薄；房間愈像北極，戶頭愈像沙漠。
夏天的物理學只有一條公式——我家的低溫，是用我帳戶的低點換來的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個冷氣一開、就感覺得到錢包在隔壁房間哭的朋友
variations: 冷氣每降一度，我的存款就跟著降一階，公平得令人心痛。
我家最涼的地方是房間，最荒涼的地方是我的銀行 App。

en.content:
My AC and my wallet are inversely related:
the cooler the air, the thinner the wallet; the more my room feels like the Arctic, the more my account feels like a desert.
Summer physics has only one formula — the chill in my home is paid for by the low point in my account.
en.editorNote: For the friend who can practically hear their wallet crying in the next room the second the AC turns on
variations: Every degree the AC drops, my savings drop a tier too — painfully fair.
The coolest place in my home is my bedroom; the most barren is my banking app.

items.sourceNote: 月用電 120 度以下不受影響 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
台電貼心提醒：「月用電 120 度以下的人，夏月電價完全不受影響。」
我打開帳單，第一個級距就已經被我跑完，120 度只是我這個月的開胃菜。
那行字不是寫給我看的，是寫給一種傳說中、夏天不開冷氣還能活下來的物種。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個看到「120 度以下不受影響」先笑出來、再嘆氣的朋友
variations: 120 度以下不受影響——這句話對我來說，跟氣象預報說「外太空今天很涼」一樣實用。
台電的善意級距，每年都在提醒我：我不屬於那個被保護的族群。

en.content:
The power company kindly reminds us: 'If you use under 120 kWh a month, summer rates don't affect you at all.'
I open my bill — I blew past the first tier already; 120 kWh was just this month's appetizer.
That line wasn't written for me. It was written for some legendary species that survives summer without ever turning on the AC.
en.editorNote: For the friend who laughs at 'under 120 kWh unaffected,' then sighs
variations: 'Under 120 kWh, unaffected' is about as useful to me as a forecast saying outer space is cool today.
The gentle lower tier reminds me every year that I do not belong to the protected class.

items.sourceNote: Dcard 租屋處電費一度 6.5 元 電卡一週從 2000 掉到 970 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
搬進新租屋處的第一週，我才知道房東的「電費一度六塊五」不是數字，是一種定價的藝術。
電卡儲值兩千，七天後剩九百七。
我盯著那台冷氣，第一次體會到「每一口涼風都在燒錢」是字面上的意思。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛搬進去、發現房東電費比台電貴一倍還在懷疑人生的租屋族
variations: 房東的電費單價，是這個城市裡唯一不受夏月電價限制、可以自由發揮的數字。
租屋處的冷氣不是家電，是一台把我電卡餘額吸光的吸塵器。

en.content:
My first week in the new rental, I learned that the landlord's 'electricity is twenty cents a unit' isn't a number — it's the art of pricing.
I loaded sixty bucks onto the meter card; seven days later it read twenty-nine.
I stared at the AC and finally understood that 'every cool breath burns money' was meant literally.
en.editorNote: For the renter who just moved in and is questioning life after seeing the landlord charge double the utility rate
variations: The landlord's per-unit rate is the only number in this city that summer rate caps can't touch.
The rental AC isn't an appliance — it's a vacuum cleaner sucking my meter card dry.

items.sourceNote: Dcard 開冷氣對北極熊道歉 環保罪惡感 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我按下冷氣開關之前，會先在心裡跟北極熊說聲對不起。
按下之後，再跟我的存款說聲對不起。
一個夏天下來，我道歉的次數，比我這輩子對任何人類道歉的總和還多。
結論：會用電的人有兩種罪惡感，一種對地球，一種對自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個開冷氣前要先做一輪心理掙扎、開完又有點愧疚的環保魂朋友
variations: 夏天的冷氣開關，是全世界按下去最有罪惡感的一顆按鈕。
我對北極熊的歉意是真的，但我對涼爽的需求，也是真的。

en.content:
Before I hit the AC switch, I quietly apologize to the polar bears.
After I hit it, I apologize to my savings.
Over one summer, I apologize more times than I have to any actual human in my entire life.
Conclusion: people who use electricity carry two guilts — one toward the planet, one toward themselves.
en.editorNote: For the eco-conscious friend who agonizes before turning on the AC and feels guilty after
variations: The AC switch is the most guilt-laden button on the planet to press.
My apology to the polar bears is real. So is my need to stop sweating.

items.sourceNote: Dcard 省錢版 這麼熱大家都去哪當冷氣乞丐 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
省錢版最熱門的問題：「這麼熱，大家都去哪當冷氣乞丐？」
底下答案五花八門：圖書館、百貨公司、IKEA、健身房、甚至有人說去看免費的房屋樣品屋。
我邊看邊點頭，邊把這串留言存進手機，命名為「夏季逃難路線圖」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個週末寧願在外面晃一整天、也不想回家開冷氣的省錢同好
variations: 現代人最務實的旅遊行程，是規劃一條完全不用付電費的冷氣路線。
當冷氣乞丐不丟臉，丟臉的是回家拆帳單那一刻的表情。

en.content:
The hottest question in the money-saving forum: 'It's boiling — where does everyone go to mooch AC?'
The answers pour in: libraries, department stores, IKEA, the gym, and one person even said the free model-home showrooms.
I nod along, screenshot the whole thread, and save it under 'Summer Evacuation Routes.'
en.editorNote: For the frugal friend who'd rather wander outside all weekend than run the AC at home
variations: The most practical itinerary of the modern age is a cooling route that costs zero in electricity.
Mooching AC isn't embarrassing. What's embarrassing is your face the moment you open the bill at home.

items.sourceNote: Dcard 爸媽有錢其他方面不吝嗇但死不開冷氣 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我爸媽其他方面都很大方，唯獨冷氣，是他們守護一生的最後防線。
買水果可以整箱搬，出國旅遊眼睛不眨，但只要我一開冷氣，整個家的氣壓瞬間下降三度，比冷氣還有效。
結論：他們不是不捨得花錢，是捨不得花在「會涼」這件事上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個夏天回家、伸手要開冷氣前都要先觀察爸媽臉色的成年子女
variations: 我爸對冷氣的態度，是這個家唯一通膨打不倒的堅持。
在我家，開冷氣不是按一個鈕，是一場需要先報備、再說服的家庭協商。

en.content:
My parents are generous about everything except the AC, which is the final line they've defended their whole lives.
They'll haul home a whole crate of fruit, book an overseas trip without blinking — but the second I turn on the AC, the air pressure in the house drops three degrees, faster than the AC ever could.
Conclusion: it's not that they hate spending money. They hate spending it on 'being cool.'
en.editorNote: For the grown kid who has to read their parents' faces before reaching for the AC remote at home
variations: My dad's stance on the AC is the one thing in this house that inflation could never shake.
In my home, turning on the AC isn't pressing a button — it's a family negotiation requiring advance notice and a persuasive case.

items.sourceNote: 2026 HVAC meme AC bill arrives heart attack register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
夏天我家有兩台機器在運轉：
一台是冷氣，負責讓房間降溫；
一台是我的心臟，負責在帳單寄到那天，自動加速到讓我懷疑要不要叫救護車。
它們最大的差別是：冷氣可以調溫度，我的心跳不行。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每次拆帳單前都要先把手放在胸口、深呼吸三次的朋友
variations: 夏天最耗電的家電是冷氣，最耗心臟的，是它寄來的那張帳單。
我的冷氣負責降溫，我的血壓負責升溫，兩者在帳單那天正式交班。

en.content:
In summer, two machines run in my home:
one is the AC, in charge of cooling the room;
the other is my heart, in charge of spiking on bill day until I wonder whether to call an ambulance.
The key difference: the AC has a temperature setting. My heart rate does not.
en.editorNote: For the friend who places a hand on their chest and takes three deep breaths before every bill
variations: The most power-hungry appliance in summer is the AC. The most heart-hungry is the bill it sends.
My AC handles the cooling, my blood pressure handles the heating, and they hand off the shift on bill day.
